member.name: M:MSStore.API.Packaged.StorePackagedAPI.#ctor(MSStore.API.Models.StoreConfigurations,System.String,System.String,System.String,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: An instance of ClientConfiguration that contains all parameters populated
The client secret of the Microsoft Entra Application that is registered to call Store APIs
The DevCenter URL used to make the API calls.
The Scope from DevCenter that will be used to request the access token.
ILogger for logs.

member.name: M:MSStore.API.Packaged.StorePackagedAPI.#ctor(MSStore.API.Models.StoreConfigurations,System.Security.Cryptography.X509Certificates.X509Certificate2,System.String,System.String,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: An instance of ClientConfiguration that contains all parameters populated
The client certificate of the Microsoft Entra Application that is registered to call Store APIs
The DevCenter URL used to make the API calls.
The Scope from DevCenter that will be used to request the access token.
ILogger for logs.

member.param: An instance of ClientConfiguration that contains all parameters populated
The DevCenter URL used to make the API calls.
The Scope from DevCenter that will be used to request the access token.
ILogger for logs.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: An instance of ClientConfiguration that contains all parameters populated
The client secret of the Microsoft Entra Application that is registered to call Store APIs
The Store API URL used to make the API calls.
The Scope from the Store APIs that will be used to request the access token.
ILogger for logs.

member.name: M:MSStore.API.StoreAPI.#ctor(MSStore.API.Models.StoreConfigurations,System.Security.Cryptography.X509Certificates.X509Certificate2,System.String,System.String,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: An instance of ClientConfiguration that contains all parameters populated
The client certificate of the Microsoft Entra Application that is registered to call Store APIs
The Store API URL used to make the API calls.
The Scope from the Store APIs that will be used to request the access token.
ILogger for logs.

member.name: T:MSStore.API.SubmissionClient
member.summary: This class is a proxy that abstracts the functionality of the API service

member.name: M:MSStore.API.SubmissionClient.#ctor(Microsoft.Identity.Client.AuthenticationResult,System.String,System.Net.Http.HttpClient)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The access token. This is JWT a token obtained from Azure Active Directory allowing the caller to invoke the API on behalf of a user
The service URL.
An HttpClient object to be used in the internal requests. Can be used for mocking.

member.name: P:MSStore.API.SubmissionClient.DefaultHeaders
member.summary: Gets or Sets the default headers.

member.name: M:MSStore.API.SubmissionClient.Dispose
member.summary: Performs application-defined tasks associated with freeing, releasing, or resetting unmanaged resources.

member.name: M:MSStore.API.SubmissionClient.GetClientCredentialAccessTokenAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Gets the authorization token for the provided client id, client secret, and the scope. This token is usually valid for 1 hour, so if your submission takes longer than that to complete, make sure to get a new one periodically.
member.param: The tenantId used to get the access token, specific to your Azure Active Directory app. Example: "d454d300-128e-2d81-334a-27d9b2baf002"
Client Id of your Azure Active Directory app. Example: "ba3c223b-03ab-4a44-aa32-38aa10c27e32"
Client secret of your Azure Active Directory app
Scope. If not provided, default one is used for the production API endpoint.
ILogger for logs.
Cancelation token.
member.returns: Autorization token. Prepend it with "Bearer: " and pass it in the request header as the value for "Authorization: " header.

member.name: M:MSStore.API.SubmissionClient.GetClientCredentialAccessTokenAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Security.Cryptography.X509Certificates.X509Certificate2,System.String,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Gets the authorization token for the provided client id, client secret, and the scope. This token is usually valid for 1 hour, so if your submission takes longer than that to complete, make sure to get a new one periodically.
member.param: The tenantId used to get the access token, specific to your Azure Active Directory app. Example: "d454d300-128e-2d81-334a-27d9b2baf002"
Client Id of your Azure Active Directory app. Example: "ba3c223b-03ab-4a44-aa32-38aa10c27e32"
Client certificate of your Azure Active Directory app
Scope. If not provided, default one is used for the production API endpoint.
ILogger for logs.
Cancelation token.
member.returns: Autorization token. Prepend it with "Bearer: " and pass it in the request header as the value for "Authorization: " header.

member.name: M:MSStore.API.SubmissionClient.InvokeAsync``1(System.Net.Http.HttpMethod,System.String,System.Object,System.Text.Json.Serialization.Metadata.JsonTypeInfo{``0},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Invokes the specified HTTP method.
member.typeparam: The return type expected by the HTTP return.
member.param: The HTTP method.
The relative URL.
Content of the request.
The type information for JSON serialization.
Cancelation token.

member.name: M:MSStore.API.SubmissionClient.SetRequest(System.Net.Http.HttpRequestMessage,System.Object)
member.summary: Sets the request.
member.param: The request.
Content of the request.
